title: Multi-thread contact depth doubled across the book this quarter
anchor: Average contacts per account sits at 4.6 this quarter, up from 2.3 last quarter.
connect: 38 of 47 accounts now carry 4-plus engaged contacts, compared to 18 of 47 last quarter.
body: Average contacts per account sits at 4.6 this quarter, up from 2.3 last quarter. 38 of 47 accounts now carry 4-plus engaged contacts, compared to 18 of 47 last quarter.
trace: Multi-threading signal shows significant improvement in relationship breadth across the book, with most accounts crossing the 4-contact renewal protection threshold.

title: Champion re-engagement events tripled in April
anchor: 9 accounts where champions had gone 14-plus days silent received confirmed responses in April.
connect: 7 of those 9 accounts crossed back to green health score within 21 days of re-engagement.
body: 9 accounts where champions had gone 14-plus days silent received confirmed responses in April. 7 of those 9 accounts crossed back to green health score within 21 days of re-engagement.
trace: Champion gap recovery pattern showing strong success rate when proactive outreach catches silent periods early.

title: Strategic stakeholder maps hit 84% currency in April
anchor: 21 of 25 strategic accounts show updated stakeholder maps within the last 30 days.
connect: February sat at 14 of 25 accounts with fresh maps.
body: 21 of 25 strategic accounts show updated stakeholder maps within the last 30 days. February sat at 14 of 25 accounts with fresh maps.
trace: Stakeholder map hygiene showing consistent improvement, critical for renewal preparation and expansion identification.

title: Two-week outreach coverage reaches book-wide high
anchor: 44 of 47 active accounts show CSM-logged interactions within the past 14 days.
connect: This marks the highest two-week coverage since Q4 2025.
body: 44 of 47 active accounts show CSM-logged interactions within the past 14 days. This marks the highest two-week coverage since Q4 2025.
trace: Outreach rhythm consistency at highest level recorded, indicating strong account management discipline across the book.

title: Q3 renewal pipeline carries 14 of 18 accounts with executive touch
anchor: 18 accounts in Q3 renewal cohort represent $1.8M total ARR.
connect: Executive sponsor interactions logged in the last 30 days for 14 of those 18 accounts.
body: 18 accounts in Q3 renewal cohort represent $1.8M total ARR. Executive sponsor interactions logged in the last 30 days for 14 of those 18 accounts.
trace: Q3 renewal preparation showing strong executive-level engagement, which correlates with 89% close rates versus 61% without.

title: Early-renewal pull-forward delivers $420K in Q2
anchor: 6 accounts originally tracking to Q3 or Q4 closed early renewals in Q2.
connect: 5 of those 6 had been flagged green for two consecutive months before the early-commit conversation.
body: 6 accounts originally tracking to Q3 or Q4 closed early renewals in Q2. 5 of those 6 had been flagged green for two consecutive months before the early-commit conversation.
trace: Early renewal pattern showing health score consistency as strong predictor of pull-forward readiness.

title: Mid-market GRR jumps to 94% in Q2
anchor: 31 of 33 mid-market accounts renewed in Q2, up from 28 of 33 in Q1.
connect: ICP-aligned mid-market accounts carried 86% of the Q2 improvement.
body: 31 of 33 mid-market accounts renewed in Q2, up from 28 of 33 in Q1. ICP-aligned mid-market accounts carried 86% of the Q2 improvement.
trace: Mid-market retention showing significant quarter-over-quarter improvement driven primarily by ICP-fit accounts.

title: Lighthouse buying committee expands from 3 to 6 contacts
anchor: April 22 QBR added COO and two business unit leads to the buying group.
connect: Buying committees that grow during renewal windows close 28 points higher than those that stay flat.
body: April 22 QBR added COO and two business unit leads to the buying group. Buying committees that grow during renewal windows close 28 points higher than those that stay flat.
trace: Lighthouse account showing expansion of decision-making influence, a strong predictor of renewal success and expansion potential.

title: 7 accounts cross feature-adoption ceiling in April
anchor: Accounts hit 80% utilization of their current tier features, representing $290K expansion potential.
connect: 4 of the 7 accounts carry logged executive sponsors on expansion conversation tracks.
body: Accounts hit 80% utilization of their current tier features, representing $290K expansion potential. 4 of the 7 accounts carry logged executive sponsors on expansion conversation tracks.
trace: Feature utilization ceiling signals showing structural readiness for tier expansion across multiple accounts.

title: Multi-thread expansion depth triples to 12 accounts
anchor: 12 active accounts now carry 5-plus engaged contacts in CRM, up from 4 last quarter.
connect: Accounts at 5-plus contacts expand at 30% within 12 months versus 5% at single-thread.
body: 12 active accounts now carry 5-plus engaged contacts in CRM, up from 4 last quarter. Accounts at 5-plus contacts expand at 30% within 12 months versus 5% at single-thread.
trace: Deep relationship breadth showing strong growth, correlating with significantly higher expansion conversion rates.

title: Usage spike signals surface 8 accounts in past 14 days
anchor: 8 accounts crossed 40% week-over-week usage increase threshold.
connect: 6 of those 8 carry active expansion tracks in CRM currently.
body: 8 accounts crossed 40% week-over-week usage increase threshold. 6 of those 8 carry active expansion tracks in CRM currently.
trace: Usage spike detection showing high correlation with existing expansion conversations, indicating signal validity for expansion timing.

title: Strategic QBR completion with value stories hits 88%
anchor: 22 of 25 strategic accounts completed April QBRs with value-story summaries logged in CRM.
connect: February QBR completion with value capture sat at 64%.
body: 22 of 25 strategic accounts completed April QBRs with value-story summaries logged in CRM. February QBR completion with value capture sat at 64%.
trace: QBR quality showing significant improvement in value documentation, which correlates with 18-point higher renewal commit rates.

title: Time to first value drops to 41 days in Q2
anchor: 11 Q2 onboardings reached first activated success criterion at median 41 days.
connect: Q1 median across 9 onboardings ran at 67 days.
body: 11 Q2 onboardings reached first activated success criterion at median 41 days. Q1 median across 9 onboardings ran at 67 days.
trace: Onboarding acceleration showing significant improvement, with faster time-to-value correlating with 26-point higher renewal rates.

title: CRM handoff completeness reaches 79% in Q2
anchor: 19 of 24 Q2 deals carried full stakeholder maps, buying reasons, and verbal commitments at handoff.
connect: Q1 handoff completeness sat at 11 of 27 deals.
body: 19 of 24 Q2 deals carried full stakeholder maps, buying reasons, and verbal commitments at handoff. Q1 handoff completeness sat at 11 of 27 deals.
trace: Sales-to-CS handoff quality showing substantial improvement, enabling 19-day faster activation for complete handoffs.

title: Account renewal correlation shows 4-contact protection threshold
anchor: Accounts at 4-plus engaged contacts renew at 91% versus 67% at 1-2 contacts.
connect: 38 of 47 accounts now sit above the 4-contact threshold this quarter.
body: Accounts at 4-plus engaged contacts renew at 91% versus 67% at 1-2 contacts. 38 of 47 accounts now sit above the 4-contact threshold this quarter.
trace: Multi-threading correlation analysis showing clear renewal protection benefit at 4-contact depth, with majority of book now above threshold.

title: Executive engagement drives 89% renewal close rates
anchor: Renewals with executive sponsor touch in 90 days before close convert at 89%.
connect: Renewals without executive engagement close at 61%.
body: Renewals with executive sponsor touch in 90 days before close convert at 89%. Renewals without executive engagement close at 61%.
trace: Executive engagement correlation showing 28-point renewal rate advantage when sponsor relationships are active in pre-renewal period.

title: Fresh stakeholder maps correlate with 22-point renewal lift
anchor: Strategic accounts with updated stakeholder maps within 30 days renew 22 points higher.
connect: Current strategic tier shows 21 of 25 accounts with fresh maps.
body: Strategic accounts with updated stakeholder maps within 30 days renew 22 points higher. Current strategic tier shows 21 of 25 accounts with fresh maps.
trace: Stakeholder map currency showing strong predictive value for renewal success, with current strategic tier well-positioned.

title: Case study advocate pool doubles to 11 accounts
anchor: 11 named accounts carry logged reference willingness in Q2, up from 5 in February.
connect: 4 case-study commits from 4 distinct accounts delivered in Q2.
body: 11 named accounts carry logged reference willingness in Q2, up from 5 in February. 4 case-study commits from 4 distinct accounts delivered in Q2.
trace: Customer advocate pipeline showing healthy growth, with active case study production indicating strong satisfaction levels.

title: Usage spikes correlate with 33% faster expansion cycles
anchor: Accounts with 40% week-over-week usage increases before expansion conversations close expansion deals 33% faster.
connect: Current usage spike cohort shows 6 of 8 accounts with active expansion tracks.
body: Accounts with 40% week-over-week usage increases before expansion conversations close expansion deals 33% faster. Current usage spike cohort shows 6 of 8 accounts with active expansion tracks.
trace: Usage spike timing correlation showing significant expansion cycle acceleration when engagement increases precede sales conversations.

title: 45-day activation threshold drives 26-point retention advantage
anchor: Accounts hitting first success criterion within 45 days renew 26 points higher than slower activations.
connect: Current Q2 median time-to-value sits at 41 days across 11 onboardings.
body: Accounts hitting first success criterion within 45 days renew 26 points higher than slower activations. Current Q2 median time-to-value sits at 41 days across 11 onboardings.
